scenario_description: User asks agent to add a disclaimer at the end, but agent puts it at the beginning. User corrects.

command:
printf 'Company Policy Document

1. Working Hours
Standard working hours are 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM, Monday through Friday.

2. Remote Work
Employees may work remotely up to 2 days per week with manager approval.

3. Leave Policy
Annual leave: 15 days. Sick leave: 10 days. Personal leave: 3 days.
' > /home/user/Desktop/policy_doc.txt

DISPLAY=:1 gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.screensaver idle-activation-enabled false 2>/dev/null; DISPLAY=:1 gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.session idle-delay 0 2>/dev/null; DISPLAY=:1 xset s off 2>/dev/null; DISPLAY=:1 xset -dpms 2>/dev/null; true
DISPLAY=:1 xdotool key Escape 2>/dev/null; sleep 0.3; DISPLAY=:1 xdotool search --class 'soffice' windowfocus windowraise 2>/dev/null || DISPLAY=:1 wmctrl -xa 'soffice.Soffice' 2>/dev/null || true

phases.instruction: Add a disclaimer to the document: 'This policy is subject to annual review.'
Wait, I wanted the disclaimer at the END of the document, not where you put it. Please move it to the very last line.
Now also add 'Last updated: March 2025' right before the disclaimer. Save.

include: This policy is subject to annual review
